heading: Art. 17. Jurisdiction of courts-martial in general
label: 817 Art. 17. Jurisdiction of courts-martial in general
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)817(a)817(b)50:577(a).50:577(b).(Art. 17), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 114In subsection (a), the word “has” is substituted for the words “shall have”.In subsection (b), the word “after” is substituted for the words “subsequent to”. The words “the provisions of” are omitted as surplusage. The words “department that includes the” are inserted before the words “armed force”, since the review is carried out by the department and not by the armed force.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)817(a)817(b)50:577(a).50:577(b).(Art. 17), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 114In subsection (a), the word “has” is substituted for the words “shall have”.In subsection (b), the word “after” is substituted for the words “subsequent to”. The words “the provisions of” are omitted as surplusage. The words “department that includes the” are inserted before the words “armed force”, since the review is carried out by the department and not by the armed force.

children.label: a
children.text: Each armed force has court-martial jurisdiction over all persons subject to this chapter. The exercise of jurisdiction by one armed force over personnel of another armed force shall be in accordance with regulations prescribed by the President.

children.label: b
children.text: In all cases, departmental review after that by the officer with authority to convene a general court-martial for the command which held the trial, where that review is required under this chapter, shall be carried out by the department that includes the armed force of which the accused is a member.
